page: Microsoft Azure DC16eds_v5 is a member of the DCEDV5 server family, utilizing dedicated Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture. This virtual machine is configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

For storage requirements, the instance includes 474 GB of local disk storage and is premium storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. It does not feature any integrated graphics processing units and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This server is classified under the memory-optimized category within its family, making it suitable for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources and local storage capacity. Typical applications include database hosting, enterprise applications, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from dedicated Intel CPU cores and local scratch space.
description: Microsoft Azure DC16eds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DCEDV5 server family, powered by dedicated Intel processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 474 GB of local disk storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Positioned as a memory-optimized resource within its family, this server is designed for workloads such as database management, enterprise applications, and general-purpose computing tasks that require dedicated CPU allocation and local storage capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC16eds_v5 is a DCEDV5 family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 474 GB of local storage, optimized for database and enterprise workloads.

tagline: An Intel-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated compute cores, balanced memory allocation, and local storage for enterprise workloads.
